steps.title: Read the graphed line
steps.content: The line shown is $y=f(x)-19$. Use two clear points from the graph in a Desmos table: $(-5,-3)$ and $(0,-8)$. A linear regression gives slope $-1$ and intercept $-8$, so the shown line is $y=-x-8$.

steps.title: Match to the form with positive $c,d$
steps.content: Rewrite as $f(x)=11-x=d-cx$ with $d=11$ and $c=1$, both positive. That matches choice A.
